name: Family Tree Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (180°C). Line a cookie sheet with foil.
2. In a large bowl, combine brown sugar, Crisco shortening, water, and vanilla extract. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended. Add eggs and beat again.
3. Sift together flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix into the creamed mixture at low speed just until blended. Stir in chocolate chips.
4. Refrigerate dough for at least one hour. Keep refrigerated until ready to use. Roll dough into ropes 1/2 inch (1 cm) in diameter. Join 2 or 3 ropes to form tree trunk on the cookie sheet. Shape remaining dough on foil-lined sheets into tree branches.
5. Bake at 375°F (180°C) for 9 to 11 minutes, or until set. Do not overbake. Cool 2 minutes on baking sheet. Remove cookies with foil and cool completely.
6. For icing, combine powdered sugar and 3 tablespoons water in a bowl. Mix to blend well. Add additional water, if needed, to reach desired consistency. Add more powdered sugar if icing is too thin.
7. Pipe names onto branches of the trees using a pastry bag fitted with a small round writing tip. If desired, tint remaining icing green and ice sugar cookie leaves. Attach leaves to branches with icing.
Makes: 2 tree cookies
